title: Add Terraform input guardrails
goal: Fail fast on Terraform inputs that would break the first GCP apply or drift away from the initial low-cost release target.
scope: Validate GCP project, region, zone, resource prefix, domain, backup bucket, SSH CIDRs, and budget inputs.
Keep the first release constrained to Free-Tier-friendly regions, e2-micro, pd-standard, and a 10-30 GB boot disk.
Close first-deploy SSH blockers by wiring deploy public key installation and host fingerprint verification.
Document the direct SSH firewall requirement for GitHub-hosted deployments.
Document that relaxing the guardrails is an explicit cost decision.
verification: Terraform validate passes with backend disabled.
Invalid low-cost inputs fail through variable validation.
Deploy workflow fails early when required SSH secrets are missing.
Terraform catches mismatched region and zone values before apply.
completed_notes: Added variable validation blocks in infra/gcp/variables.tf.
Added optional DEPLOY_PUBLIC_KEY installation to the VM bootstrap script.
Added SSH_FINGERPRINT validation and appleboy host fingerprint verification to deploy jobs.
Documented the SSH firewall requirement for GitHub-hosted deploy runners.
Documented the initial cost guardrails in the Terraform and GCP deployment guides.
Registered TASK-022 in the task tracker.
